reasoning: The goal set by the Central Bank of Nigeria to reach 95% financial inclusion by 2024 was widely seen as an incredibly ambitious, likely aspirational target. According to the World Bank's Global Findex 2021, only about 45% of Nigerian adults had an account at a financial institution or mobile money provider. More recently, the EFInA Access to Financial Services in Nigeria 2023 survey estimated formal financial inclusion at around 64%. Achieving a jump from ~64% to 95% within one year is structurally virtually impossible given systemic barriers like poverty, lack of official identification (many adults still lack a NIN or BVN), and poor infrastructure in rural areas. Even if the CBN were to publish highly inflated numbers, the resolution criteria specify that data known to be inaccurate will be overridden by World Bank or credible independent researchers (like EFInA). Therefore, the probability of legitimately hitting the 95% mark before 2025 is extremely low. I am setting the probability to the lower allowed bound of 0.05.
